title: Confocal Microscopy of DFP-Treated Cells
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"abg88","text":"This protocol details the confocal microscopy of DFP-Treated cells after fixation and staining.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Seed the cells on glass coverslips (12 mm #1.5) at a concentration of 120,000 cells/well, and after treatment with DFP for 24:00:00 , fix in 100% methanol for 00:20:00 at -20 °C .
After washing with PBS, block the samples with 5% (v/v) BSA (9048-46-8, Sigma-Aldrich) diluted in PBS for 01:00:00 at Room temperature .
Dilute the primary and secondary antibodies in 5% BSA and incubate for 01:00:00 at Room temperature with three PBS washing steps in between.
Mount the cells on microscopy slides in DAPI Fluoromount-G mounting medium (0100-20, Southern Biotech), which stains the nuclei, add a cover glass, and store at 4 °C until use.

Perform the confocal microscopy with a Zeiss LSM700 laser scanning confocal microscopy with Plan-Apochromat 40×/1.30 Oil DIC, WD 0.21 mm objective or Zeiss LSM900 with Airyscan 2 confocal super-resolution microscopy with Plan-Apochromat 63×/1.4 Oil DIC, WD 0.19 mm objective.
creator: Elias Adriaenssens
